title: feat(runtime): add AtUri.parse() / parseOrNull() helpers
description:
Add a structural AT URI parser to the AtUri value class so consumers stop reinventing string-splitting at every call site that needs (repo, collection, rkey, fragment). Hit by nubecita's like/repost data layer (DefaultLikeRepostRepository.parseAtUri) and will be hit by every record-uri-keyed write path (likes, reposts, follows, blocks, lists, replies addressed by parent uri, profile edits).

Source: GitHub issue #57 — https://github.com/kikin81/atproto-kotlin/issues/57
Spec: https://atproto.com/specs/at-uri-scheme
File target: runtime/src/commonMain/kotlin/io/github/kikin81/atproto/runtime/AtUriParser.kt (new)
Test target: runtime/src/commonTest/kotlin/io/github/kikin81/atproto/runtime/AtUriParserTest.kt (new)
Sibling precedent: kikinlex-dmk (Datetime.toInstant), RecordDecoder.kt / RecordDecoderTest.kt
design:
Structural validation only — non-empty segments. Semantic validity of the repo segment (DID/handle syntax) belongs to the Did / Handle / AtIdentifier wrapper types in a separate ticket; the parser composes for free once those validators exist. Putting heuristic identifier checks in the parser duplicates the concern and means touching the parser every time identifier rules evolve.

Fragment returned as bare suffix (no leading #) — matches java.net.URI.getFragment() and every URL-ish parser people will compare against. KDoc must spell out: "leading # stripped; null when absent."

No String companion overload. Call sites already hold AtUri (every generated type uses it); AtUri(raw).parseOrNull() covers the raw-string case in one extra wrap. Adding a companion fun doubles the API + test matrix for one character of convenience. YAGNI — promote it when a consumer actually asks.

Dedicated AtUriParser.kt rather than appending to StringFormats.kt. StringFormats.kt is 100% value-class declarations today; a 50-LOC parser changes its character. Single-responsibility per file matches RecordDecoder.kt precedent.
acceptance_criteria:
- public data class AtUriParts(repo: AtIdentifier, collection: Nsid?, rkey: RecordKey?, fragment: String?) defined in AtUriParser.kt
- public fun AtUri.parse(): AtUriParts — throws IllegalArgumentException with a descriptive message on structural failure
- public fun AtUri.parseOrNull(): AtUriParts? — returns null on structural failure, never throws
- KDoc on each public symbol; links to https://atproto.com/specs/at-uri-scheme; documents that fragment is bare (no #) and null when absent
- Unit tests in AtUriParserTest.kt covering the matrix in the notes
- ./gradlew :runtime:jvmTest passes
- ./gradlew spotlessCheck passes
notes:
Test matrix (explicit):
- Full URI: at://did:plc:abc/app.bsky.feed.post/3lkrkey → repo, collection, rkey set; fragment null
- Repo-only: at://did:plc:abc → repo only
- Repo+collection (no rkey): at://did:plc:abc/app.bsky.feed.post
- Trailing slash: at://did:plc:abc/ normalizes to repo-only (do NOT reject)
- Fragment on full URI: at://did:plc:abc/app.bsky.feed.post/3l#main → fragment="main"
- Fragment without record: at://did:plc:abc#frag → repo + fragment set, collection/rkey null (valid per spec)
- Handle as repo: at://alice.bsky.social/app.bsky.feed.post/3l → accepted (no semantic check)
- Reject (parse throws, parseOrNull returns null): missing at:// prefix; empty repo (at:///); empty path segments (at://did:plc:x//rkey)

Out of scope / follow-ups:
- Spec-correct Did / Handle / AtIdentifier syntax validators — separate ticket; the parser is intentionally agnostic so it composes for free.
- AtUriParts.toAtUri(): AtUri round-trip helper — include if it falls out naturally during implementation; otherwise punt to a follow-up issue. Not a blocker for this ticket.

Branch: feat/<bd-id>-aturi-parse per the project's <type>/<bd-id>-<slug> convention.

title: feat(ci): add JaCoCo coverage on :runtime and :oauth
description:
Test counts on the consumer-impacting hand-written modules are lean (7 files in :runtime, 4 in :oauth as of 2026-04-27). With Renovate now pushing dep bumps regularly, we need visibility into coverage gaps so silent regressions in serialization, value-class parsing, or DPoP crypto don't slip through.

Apply JaCoCo plugin to :runtime + :oauth build.gradle.kts. Configure jacocoTestReport to emit both HTML (for browsing) and XML (for future Codecov integration). Wire jacocoCoverageVerification as a CI gate.

Threshold-setting strategy: do NOT pick an arbitrary number. Generate the first report on the current main, observe per-module line coverage, set the floor at current_coverage - 2-3%. That way the first commit doesn't fail CI and we can ratchet upward over time as gaps get filled.

Scope deliberately narrow:
- :runtime (consumer-facing core: XrpcClient, AtField, OpenUnion, value classes — biggest blast radius)
- :oauth (DPoP + PAR + PKCE crypto — security-sensitive)
- Skip :models (generated, golden-tested at the source — coverage of generated code is a misleading metric)
- Skip :generator (already covered by GoldenFileTest, which is stronger than line coverage for this workload)
- Skip :compose / :compose-material3 (UI helpers, smaller surface — can add later if Renovate breakage hits there)
acceptance_criteria:
- JaCoCo plugin applied to :runtime + :oauth build.gradle.kts (or via convention plugin if one exists)
- ./gradlew :runtime:jacocoTestReport :oauth:jacocoTestReport produces HTML + XML in build/reports/jacoco/
- CI uploads HTML report as a workflow artifact (so reviewers can download and browse)
- jacocoCoverageVerification gates CI at the measured floor (TBD; document in PR body what the floor was set to and why)
- README or CONTRIBUTING.md gets a one-line note on viewing the coverage report
notes: Touches: :runtime/build.gradle.kts, :oauth/build.gradle.kts, .github/workflows/ci.yaml, CONTRIBUTING.md (or README.md). Conflicts likely with the binary-compat + JDK matrix bd issues on ci.yaml — last to merge rebases.

title: feat(ci): adopt kotlinx-binary-compatibility-validator to track public API surface
description:
Renovate will start pushing dependency bumps that could silently change exposed types (e.g., a Ktor type leaking into a public signature, an accidental visibility flip during a refactor). The repo has no tracker today, so a breaking API change could ship in a patch release.

Apply the kotlinx binary-compatibility-validator plugin at the root build.gradle.kts. Run ./gradlew apiDump once to baseline api/<module>.api text files for every published module. Commit those files. CI then runs ./gradlew apiCheck on every PR and fails on unexpected diff. Updating public API requires a deliberate ./gradlew apiDump commit, which becomes a clear review signal.

Scope:
- :runtime (KMP, JVM + iOS publications)
- :models (generated, but the surface IS the contract — guards against generator regressions slipping past golden tests at the public layer)
- :oauth (JVM)
- :compose (Android)
- :compose-material3 (Android)
- Skip :generator (JVM-only internal tool, not consumed)

Reference: https://github.com/Kotlin/binary-compatibility-validator
acceptance_criteria:
- Plugin applied at root build.gradle.kts
- api/ directory committed with baseline dumps for all five published modules
- CI workflow runs ./gradlew apiCheck on every PR
- CONTRIBUTING.md notes that intentional breaking API changes require an accompanying ./gradlew apiDump commit
- spotlessCheck passes; api/*.api files don't trip the formatter (they shouldn't — text files outside src/)
notes: Touches: root build.gradle.kts (or build-logic if there's a convention plugin), per-module build.gradle.kts maybe, .github/workflows/ci.yaml, CONTRIBUTING.md. Likely conflicts on ci.yaml with kikinlex-bd issues for JaCoCo + JDK matrix — last to merge rebases.

title: DpopAuthProvider.onUnauthorized: handle stale nonce + expired access token in one 401 cycle
description:
Fixes upstream issue kikin81/atproto-kotlin#33.

When a single 401 from the PDS carries both 'use_dpop_nonce' (with a fresh DPoP-Nonce header) and the bound access token is already expired (cold-start-after-idle case, > access-token TTL), DpopAuthProvider.onUnauthorized short-circuits after storing the new nonce and returns true without calling refreshTokens(). XrpcClient performs its single retry with the same expired access token + new nonce, gets a second 401 (invalid_token), which is mapped to XrpcError.Unknown. User sees a generic error; tapping retry succeeds because by then pdsNonce is current and the next 401 falls through to refreshTokens().

The implicit contract is 'onUnauthorized recovers everything recoverable in one call' — the nonce branch is violating that.

Affected code:
- oauth/src/main/kotlin/io/github/kikin81/atproto/oauth/DpopAuthProvider.kt:63-73 (onUnauthorized short-circuit)
- runtime/src/commonMain/kotlin/io/github/kikin81/atproto/runtime/XrpcClient.kt:58-67, 89-100, 120-130 (single-retry contract — leave intact)
design:
Approach: parse access token as JWT, read exp claim, refresh proactively if expired (option 1 from the issue, preferred by the reporter — more robust than parsing WWW-Authenticate strings).

In onUnauthorized:
1. Store new nonce if present (don't early-return).
2. Decode session.accessToken as JWT: split on '.', base64url-decode payload, read exp (Long). On any failure (not a JWT, no exp claim, parse error), treat as 'cannot determine expiry' and skip the proactive refresh.
3. If exp <= now + clockOffsetSeconds + skew (30s), take refreshMutex and call refreshTokens() in the same call.
4. Return true if either action ran.

Notes:
- refreshTokens() already persists pdsNonce alongside the new tokens via session.copy, so we can skip the early persistNonces() write when we know we're about to refresh.
- Don't widen XrpcClient's retry budget — the contract is 'one chance to recover everything', not 'retry until success'.
acceptance_criteria:
- onUnauthorized called once with new nonce + expired access token recovers BOTH (nonce stored AND token refreshed) before returning true
- Existing path preserved: opaque (non-JWT) access tokens, same-nonce 401s, and pure expired-token 401s still work
- New regression test in DpopAuthProviderTest exercises the combined-recovery path against a MockEngine
- Existing AtOAuthTest.createClientRefreshesExpiredToken still passes
- spotlessCheck + ./gradlew :oauth:test green
close_reason: Merged to main via PR #34 (squash commit bd60231d), released as v5.1.2.

title: OpenSpec: lexicon-drift-automation — scheduled lexicon corpus bump workflow
description:
Upstream AT Protocol lexicons at @atproto/lex evolve continuously, but this repo has no automated way to detect drift. A maintainer must remember to run npx lex install. Two consequences: (1) invisible drift between manual bumps; (2) package.json declares '@atproto/lex': '*' — reproducibility maintained only by package-lock.json + npm ci.

Add a scheduled GitHub Actions workflow (.github/workflows/lexicon-bump.yaml) that runs weekly and on workflow_dispatch — npm update, npx lex install, regenerate models, and open a PR if anything changed. Also tighten package.json to an exact version pin and document the flow in CONTRIBUTING.md.

Source: openspec/changes/lexicon-drift-automation/
Artifacts: proposal.md, design.md, specs/, tasks.md
Capability modified: lexicon-toolchain
acceptance_criteria:
- New .github/workflows/lexicon-bump.yaml runs weekly and on workflow_dispatch
- Workflow runs npm update @atproto/lex, npx lex install, generateModels, opens PR on diff
- PR body summarizes @atproto/lex version delta, lexicons.json CID changes, and generated source delta
- at-protocol-generator/package.json pinned to exact version (no '*')
- CONTRIBUTING.md documents the automated flow + how to trigger manual run
- No runtime or generator code changes; no breaking changes to artifact surface

title: compose / compose-material3: lower minSdk from 28
description:
The :compose and :compose-material3 artifacts (introduced in PR #22, shipped in v5.1.0) declare minSdk = 28, cutting off consumers on minSdk 21/26 from adopting the helper — including projects that consume :runtime, :oauth, :models which inherit a much lower default. Encountered while wiring compose-material3:5.1.0 into nubecita.

A scan of the source (Utf8CharBoundaryTable, BlueskyTextBuilder, BlueskyAnnotatedString, RememberBlueskyAnnotatedString) shows nothing requiring API 28+. Compose UI and Material3 themselves support minSdk 21. The 28 declaration looks like an undocumented defensive default chosen at module-creation time.

Source: GitHub issue #25 — https://github.com/kikin81/atproto-kotlin/issues/25
Files: compose/build.gradle.kts:12 and compose-material3/build.gradle.kts:13
Related: #23 (stability) and #24 (test-junit catalog) from same PR review
acceptance_criteria:
- Decide on target minSdk for :compose and :compose-material3 (21 or 26 recommended)
- Update both build.gradle.kts files
- Guard with Build.VERSION.SDK_INT checks if anything needs higher API
- Add comment in each build.gradle.kts explaining choice (or update README)
- Cut a follow-on patch release (v5.1.1) so consumers can adopt without bumping their own minSdk
close_reason: Shipped via PR #28 — both :compose and :compose-material3 now declare minSdk=23 with explanatory comment. Semantic-release cut v5.1.1 on merge.

title: feat(runtime): add Datetime.toInstant() / toInstantOrNull() extensions
description:
Add KMP extensions on the Datetime value class so consumers can convert AT Protocol RFC 3339 timestamps to kotlinx.datetime.Instant without re-parsing at every call site. Requested by the nubecita team while implementing relative-time formatting for feed/profile/notification timestamps.

Source: GitHub issue #21 — https://github.com/kikin81/atproto-kotlin/issues/21
File: runtime/src/commonMain/kotlin/io/github/kikin81/atproto/runtime/StringFormats.kt:59
Sibling: kikin81/atproto-kotlin#20 (facet renderer)
acceptance_criteria:
- Datetime.toInstant(): Instant added
- Datetime.toInstantOrNull(): Instant? added
- kotlinx-datetime declared as runtime dependency (if not already)
- KDoc explains failure modes
- Unit tests cover valid RFC 3339 (Z, offset, sub-second), malformed inputs (throws/null), and round-trip

title: ci: extend binary-compat tracking to :compose and :compose-material3
description:
kotlinx-binary-compatibility-validator (introduced in PR for kikinlex-bm1) auto-discovers Kotlin JVM and KMP projects but does NOT auto-traverse Android library modules. So :runtime, :models, and :oauth are tracked, but :compose and :compose-material3 are not — gap in the same Renovate-driven safety net.

Two paths investigated:
1. Force-apply the validator plugin in each Android module's build.gradle.kts via apply(plugin = libs.plugins.binary.compatibility.validator.get().pluginId).
2. Wait for upstream support (some related issues open in the validator repo).

Path 1 is the practical option. Worth a separate PR because Android library API surface tracking is noisier (R class deps, BuildConfig, @hide markers), so the first apiDump may need filtering — keeping it scoped avoids polluting the bm1 baseline PR.

Reference: parent PR for kikinlex-bm1.
Sibling: kikinlex-bm1 (this is the follow-up).
acceptance_criteria:
- :compose and :compose-material3 each have their own api/<module>.api dump under version control
- ./gradlew :compose:apiCheck :compose-material3:apiCheck succeed
- CI's existing apiCheck step picks them up automatically (no workflow change needed)
- CONTRIBUTING.md updated to remove the 'aren't tracked yet' note
notes: Touches: compose/build.gradle.kts, compose-material3/build.gradle.kts, new api/ dirs in both modules, CONTRIBUTING.md.

title: ci: matrix-test against JDK 17 and 21
description:
CI currently uses a single JDK (17, from .java-version). Kotlin ecosystem deps that Renovate pushes increasingly require JDK 21 toolchain features (e.g., upcoming Kotlin / KSP / AGP combinations). A matrix catches incompatibilities at PR time instead of after merge.

Add strategy.matrix.java-version: [17, 21] to the test job in .github/workflows/ci.yaml. Use actions/setup-java with the matrix value. Both JDKs run in parallel; either failing fails the gate.

Keep the release job pinned to JDK 17 — there's no value in publishing artifacts twice, and Maven Central / GitHub Packages don't care about the build JDK. Lint job can stay single-JDK too (Spotless + ktlint don't depend on JDK semantics).

Reference: https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-jobs/using-a-matrix-for-your-jobs
acceptance_criteria:
- ci.yaml test job runs on both JDK 17 and JDK 21
- Both JDKs pass on current main before merge (verify locally if needed: ./gradlew test against each)
- Release job stays on JDK 17 (no double-publish)
- Lint job stays single-JDK
- Matrix uses fail-fast: false so we see results from both legs even if one fails
notes: Smallest of the three test-safeguard issues — touches only .github/workflows/ci.yaml. Conflicts likely with the binary-compat + JaCoCo bd issues on the same file; last to merge rebases.

title: README polish: Central badge, codegen mermaid diagram, contributing link, releases wording fix
description:
Four small README improvements bundled into one chore PR.

1. Add Maven Central version badge alongside the existing GitHub release badge — Central is what most consumers actually pull from.
2. Add a mermaid diagram of the codegen pipeline (Parse → Resolve → Context-tag → Name → Verify → Plan → Emit) under the Modules section. The existing prose in the :generator row is dense; a diagram makes the seven stages legible at a glance and reinforces that this is a codegen SDK. GitHub renders mermaid natively.
3. Add a Contributing section above License linking to CONTRIBUTING.md and noting that the issue + PR templates auto-apply via GitHub's New Issue / New PR flows. README currently has no signpost to CONTRIBUTING.
4. Fix README:149-150 — 'The release workflow runs a single release job' is wrong. release.yaml has lint/test/build gate jobs that run before the release job. Reword to reflect actual structure.
acceptance_criteria:
- Maven Central badge renders and links to https://central.sonatype.com/artifact/io.github.kikin81.atproto/runtime
- Mermaid pipeline diagram renders on GitHub
- README has a Contributing section linking to CONTRIBUTING.md
- Releases section accurately describes the gate-jobs → release flow
- Pre-commit hooks pass; no broken links
notes: Earlier triage walked back the 'pinned by CID in lexicons.json' finding — that file does exist in generator/, the README is correct. No fix needed there.

title: ci: adopt Renovate for automated dependency updates
description:
The repo currently has no automated dependency bot — no Renovate, no Dependabot. JVM and npm deps drift silently until someone notices. Sister repo code/nubecita has Renovate configured (.github/renovate.json) extending open-turo/renovate-config:jvm with grouping rules for kotlin-ecosystem and compose-ui, automerge for minor/patch Gradle updates, and a dependency dashboard. Mirror that setup here so kikinlex gets the same coverage.

Note: this does NOT replace the separate lexicon-drift-automation work (kikinlex-46v). Renovate's hosted app cannot run npx lex install (postUpgradeTasks is admin-only / disabled on Mend's hosted Renovate), so it can detect a new @atproto/lex npm version but cannot regenerate the lexicons.json CID snapshot — its PRs would fail npx lex install --ci in CI. The two beads cover different ground.

Reference: code/nubecita/.github/renovate.json
Sibling work: kikinlex-46v (lexicon-drift-automation — needs its own non-Renovate workflow regardless)
acceptance_criteria:
- Add .github/renovate.json mirroring nubecita's setup (extends config:recommended + open-turo/renovate-config + open-turo/renovate-config:jvm)
- Include packageRules from nubecita: kotlin-ecosystem grouping, compose-ui grouping, gradle changelog link, gradle minor/patch automerge
- Verify Renovate App is installed on the repo (or document the install step)
- Trigger an initial Renovate run via the dashboard issue and verify it opens grouped PRs as expected
- Document in CONTRIBUTING.md that dep updates flow through Renovate
close_reason: Merged to main via PR #37 (3fca192d). Renovate fired immediately and opened 10 dep PRs.

title: compose: move kotlin-test-junit dep to libs.versions.toml
description:
compose/build.gradle.kts and compose-material3/build.gradle.kts declare kotlin-test-junit with a string literal anchored to the catalog only via libs.versions.kotlin.get(). This breaks the Renovate / version-catalog single-source convention used by every other dep. Same pattern exists in samples/android/build.gradle.kts — fix all three modules in one go.

Source: GitHub issue #24 — https://github.com/kikin81/atproto-kotlin/issues/24
acceptance_criteria:
- Catalog entry kotlin-test-junit added to gradle/libs.versions.toml
- compose/build.gradle.kts uses libs.kotlin.test.junit
- compose-material3/build.gradle.kts uses libs.kotlin.test.junit
- samples/android/build.gradle.kts uses libs.kotlin.test.junit
- ./gradlew :compose:testDebugUnitTest :compose-material3:test :samples:android:testDebugUnitTest passes
close_reason: Shipped via PR #29 — kotlin-test-junit catalog entry added; :compose, :compose-material3, and :samples:android now reference libs.kotlin.test.junit. chore: commit, no release.

title: compose-material3: stabilize rememberBlueskyAnnotatedString for Compose skipping
description:
rememberBlueskyAnnotatedString takes facets: List<Facet>?, which Compose treats as unstable — the surrounding composable can't skip recomposition even with reference-equal lists. In a LazyColumn of feed posts, every parent recomposition rebuilds the boundary table and annotation graph for every visible item.

Two paths: cheap @Stable annotation, or migrate to ImmutableList<Facet> from kotlinx.collections.immutable. Recommendation in the issue is to measure first via Compose recomposition reports on the sample app's feed before choosing.

Source: GitHub issue #23 — https://github.com/kikin81/atproto-kotlin/issues/23
Related: PR #22 (where helper was introduced)
acceptance_criteria:
- Confirm via Compose recomposition reports that this is a real bottleneck (or rule out and close)
- If confirmed, add @Stable annotation OR migrate to ImmutableList<Facet> in :compose and :compose-material3 public signatures
- Update sample's toListOrNull (or new toImmutableListOrNull)
- Document the pattern in compose/README.md
